title: Police respond to threat made against Perkins School for the Blind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Watertown police responded to a threat at Perkins School for the Blind, prompting a lockdown Thursday morning.
Police and school staff determined the threat's credibility and ensured the safety of the community; the situation was resolved.
The incident will be handled internally with no criminal charges filed, Watertown police announced.

title: Second arrest made in alleged sexual abuse of dog in Connecticut
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Damon Mesite, 28, was arrested in Meriden, Connecticut for alleged involvement in sexual abuse of a dog.
Kristiina Kennedy, 45, previously charged with sexually assaulting her dog, led to investigation and searches.
Kennedy's dog examined uninjured; authorities removed her cats after finding concerning images and videos.

title: Bruins reportedly moving on from one of their assistant coaches
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are not renewing assistant coach Jay Leach's contract for the 2026 offseason.
Leach, 46, rejoined the Bruins in 2024 and was responsible for defense and penalty kill operations.
Boston's defense struggled under Leach, ranking low in defensive metrics and penalty kill success rates.

title: NFL insider would be ‘surprised’ if Patriots don’t land A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NFL insider Ian Rapoport believes it's likely the Patriots will acquire A.J. Brown, despite no current discussions.
The Eagles' financial hit from trading Brown decreases on June 1, but no deal is expected on that date.
Patriots' Kayshon Boutte is speculated to be part of a potential trade involving draft picks for A.J. Brown.

title: Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A day trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day costs approximately $162.75, including beach parking, meals, and gas.
A couple's weekend getaway on Cape Cod totals about $1,867.55, covering lodging, meals, and entertainment expenses.
A three-night family vacation on Cape Cod costs around $2,331.35, including hotel stay, meals, activities, and gas.

title: 5 arrested in brawl at Dorchester courthouse
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Five arrested after brawl at Dorchester courthouse on Wednesday morning, authorities report.
Suspects include Syncere Johnson, Shamar Passley-Clark, Terrel Stokes, and Damontay Brown, all aged 19-20.
All five face charges of affray; 17-year-old also charged with assault and battery.

title: Mass. man pleads guilty to prostitution and fraud charges over strip club activities, COVID loan
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Kenneth Denning, 69, of Holland, Massachusetts, pleaded guilty to promoting prostitution at a Connecticut strip club he owned.
Denning admitted to defrauding the IRS and fraudulently obtaining a $149,900 COVID-19 relief loan for his strip club.
Denning agreed to pay $550,000 restitution to the IRS and $150,000 to the SBA, facing a 50-year maximum sentence.

title: The Red Sox have a clear formula for success. Is it sustainable?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox have a franchise-low .240 batting average this season, lowest since 1968, and lowest ERA since 1992.
The team is 14-6 when scoring four runs or more but struggles offensively, heavily relying on strong pitching.
Historically, no Red Sox team has made playoffs with a batting average under .240 or fewer than four runs per game.

title: Alleged Memorial Drive shooter found dangerous, ordered held without bail
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Tyler Brown ordered held without bail for allegedly shooting randomly on Memorial Drive, injuring two people.
Brown faces six felony and two misdemeanor charges, including assault to murder and attempted assault.
Brown's attorney plans a robust defense; he appeared virtually for his hearing from a hotel bed.

title: Isaiah Thomas hopes ‘second act’ with Celtics will help him become a GM
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Isaiah Thomas rejoins the Boston Celtics as a scout, aiming for a future role as a team executive.
Thomas played for eight teams after leaving the Celtics in 2018 before ending his career with the Suns in 2024.
Thomas will focus on scouting efforts on the West Coast while still residing in the Seattle area.
